other_observers: Andy Boyce, Andrew Spencer, Ryan Schellpfeffer, Susan Allerton, Barb Byron; The owners of the feeders reported the bird to Jim Beatty, who originally identified it tentatively as a female Blue-throated.
specific_location: Feeders at Durango Mountain Resort and Ski Area condiminiums
description: Big hummer, at least 1.5 times size of nearby Rufous; direct comparison. In bins, with bird perched on feeder facing away, all-green iridescent back and rump were obvious. No bronzy coloration. In flight, pale tail corners were not obvious, nor were they obvious on perched bird. White postocular spot trailed by faint whitish line, but definitely not a strong white postocular stripe like you'd expect on Blue-throated. No white below the malar; throat faintly streaked with rows of darkish spots. All grayish-white below; no rufous coloration there or in tail. Bill seemed kinda longish but no guarantees on that; didn't quite get the looks at it that I'd have liked.Behaviors: Came in to feeders at 7:26 AM. Stayed about 35-40 seconds, sitting on several feeders during that time, in several different positions. Returned about five minutes later for a very brief visit.Call: bird was silentPlumage: adult
